ops.name: abs
ops.notes: Exact decomposition: `ReLU(x) + ReLU(-x)`. Any non-zero measurement here would indicate a compiler or float32 bug.

distributions.name: abs_uniform_pm50
distributions.description: Uniform signed inputs on [-50, 50]. `abs` is exact (ReLU(x) + ReLU(-x)) so any residual is float32 round-off only.

ops.name: bool_all_true
ops.notes: Single `compare` at threshold `N-1` over the sum of N ±1 inputs. Sum is N only when all are +1; otherwise ≤ N-2 — threshold cleanly separates the two, so exact on ±1 inputs.

distributions.name: bool_triple_signed
distributions.description: Three independent ±1 boolean streams — exercises all 8 truth-table rows of `bool_any_true` and `bool_all_true`.

ops.name: bool_any_true
ops.notes: Two stacked `compare` layers. On clean ±1 inputs the intermediate sum lands well outside every ramp zone, so the composition is exact.

distributions.name: bool_triple_signed
distributions.description: Three independent ±1 boolean streams — exercises all 8 truth-table rows of `bool_any_true` and `bool_all_true`.

ops.name: bool_not
ops.notes: Single `compare` with inverted levels. Exact on clean ±1 inputs; inherits `compare`'s ramp-zone error for inputs near 0.

distributions.name: bool_single_signed
distributions.description: Random ±1 inputs (discrete, no ramp-zone samples) — `bool_not` is only defined on clean boolean inputs.

ops.name: ceil_int
ops.notes: Reduction to `-floor_int(-x)`. On integer inputs every value lands in a flat zone of the floor staircase, so the op is exact here even though it inherits approximate semantics for continuous inputs.

distributions.name: ceil_integers_neg5_10
distributions.description: Integer inputs on [-5, 10]. `ceil_int(k) = k` for integer k (flat zones in the floor staircase reused under -floor(-x)).

ops.name: clamp
ops.notes: Piecewise-linear identity with 4 breakpoints at `[lo, lo + eps, hi - eps, hi]`. In the interior flat zone (this distribution) the op is a pure identity; boundary samples would fall inside the `eps`-wide ramp and lose precision but are out of scope for this negative-control distribution.

distributions.name: clamp_flat_zone_pm20
distributions.description: Uniform samples on [-8, 8] with clamp bounds [-10, 10] — every sample is inside the flat identity zone. Residual measures float32 round-off only.

ops.name: compare
ops.notes: ReLU-pair ramp. Inputs within `1/step_sharpness` of the threshold land inside the ramp zone and yield an interpolated value; inputs further away match the step function exactly. The `compare_near_thresh_*` distributions deliberately stress the ramp — the reference is a discrete step function, so samples inside the ramp legitimately report large error.

distributions.name: compare_near_thresh_0
distributions.description: Samples within ±0.3 of the threshold at 0.0 — stresses the 2-neuron ramp zone (width 1/step_sharpness).

distributions.name: compare_uniform_pm80
distributions.description: Uniform samples on [-80, 80] — measures compare's ramp error well away from the threshold (expected ≈ 0).

ops.name: cond_gate
ops.notes: `big_offset` ReLU gate. On clean ±1 conditions the output is exactly `inp` (cond=+1) or `0` (cond=-1) up to float32 round-off; noisy conditions blur the gate.

distributions.name: cond_gate_signed_bool_times_value
distributions.description: ±1 conditions paired with scalar values in [-5, 5]. Expected output: `value` when cond=+1, `0` when cond=-1.

ops.name: equals_vector
ops.notes: Single-ReLU approximate equality test. Inside the `1/embedding_step_sharpness`-wide transition ball around the target the output interpolates between -1 and +1.

distributions.name: equals_vector_match_and_off
distributions.description: Half exact-match against target vector [1, 2, 3], half random vectors in [-3, 3]³ — measures the ReLU-based equality test's margin of separation between matches and non-matches.

ops.name: exp
ops.notes: Natural exponential via piecewise-linear interpolation with uniform breakpoint spacing. Constant relative output error per cell `(Δx)²/8` because `d²exp/dx² = exp`. Pairs with `log` to implement `A·B = exp(log A + log B)` and `A/B = exp(log A − log B)` in log-space chains.

distributions.name: exp_pm5
distributions.description: exp(x) over [-5, 5] — output spans [exp(-5), exp(5)] ≈ [0.0067, 148.4], the natural pairing for log over [0.01, 100]. Uniform 256-BP grid (`Δx ≈ 0.0392`); per-cell relative-error bound is `(Δx)²/8 ≈ 1.9e-4`.

ops.name: floor_int
ops.notes: Staircase with ramp width `1/step_sharpness` directly below each integer. Inputs near integer boundaries land in the ramp zone and produce interpolated values; callers with integer-ish inputs should use `thermometer_floor_div` instead, whose ramps sit at half-integer thresholds.

distributions.name: floor_near_boundary_10
distributions.description: Samples within ±1/step_sharpness of integer k=5 — stresses the ramp zone where `floor_int` interpolates.

distributions.name: floor_uniform_neg5_10
distributions.description: Uniform continuous samples on [-5, 10] — most land in flat zones; measures baseline `floor_int` accuracy.

ops.name: linear_bin_index
ops.notes: End-to-end composite (~6 MLP sublayers). Error compounds across `reciprocal`, `signed_multiply`, `clamp`, and the terminal `thermometer_floor_div`. Inputs close to integer bin boundaries land in the staircase's ramp zone; `_bin_index_ref` uses `torch.floor` on the exact linear mapping, so tight bin edges reveal ramp-zone slippage.

distributions.name: linear_bin_index_tex_col_16
distributions.description: 16-bin quantisation of x ∈ [0, 64] with x_min=0, x_max=64 — mirrors texture-column bucketing in the RENDER stage.

ops.name: log
ops.notes: Natural log via per-section piecewise-linear interpolation. The op auto-sections the input range geometrically by `section_factor=10` (default decades) and routes via thermometer compare + multiply_2d blending, so float32 cancellation is bounded by section width regardless of overall input range. Pairs with `exp` for log-space arithmetic chains.

distributions.name: log_4decades_001_100
distributions.description: log(x) over [0.01, 100] — 4 decades. With per-decade sectioning (default), each section's pre-cancellation magnitude is bounded by `section_factor=10`, so float32 ULP noise is ~1.2e-6 per section. The dominant residual error comes from the multiply_2d blending grid in routing.

distributions.name: log_6decades_wide
distributions.description: log(x) over [0.01, 30000] — 6 decades. Stresses the sectioning path: a single-section piecewise log over this range fails outright (cancellation floor `(x_max/x_min) · 2⁻²³ ≈ 0.36 absolute, observed worst ~1.0 abs). Sectioning drops the floor to ~5e-3 absolute, dominated by compare-cancellation noise at large `x` propagating through multiply_2d blending.

ops.name: log_abs
ops.notes: Fused `log(clamp(|x|, min_abs, max_abs))` for signed input. Single-piecewise V-shape over signed `x` for `max_abs/min_abs ≤ 10⁴` (1 sublayer); falls back to `abs + sectioned log` for wider ratios (3 sublayers). Pairs with `exp` and Linear addition for log-domain multiplication of a signed by a positive value.

distributions.name: log_abs_3decades_pm100
distributions.description: log_abs(x) for x ∈ [-100, 100] with min_abs=0.1, max_abs=100 (3 decades on |x|). Signed log-uniform |x| samples plus a V-bottom grid concentrated in [-0.2, 0.2] to stress the flat-zone transition at ±min_abs. Single-piecewise path (ratio 1000 < single-piecewise threshold).

ops.name: low_rank_2d
ops.notes: Rank-3 SVD of `atan(cross/dot)` on a non-uniform grid. Worst-cell error is bounded by σ_{K+1}, the first truncated singular value — verify against the SVD computed in `tests/ops/test_low_rank_2d.py:100`.

distributions.name: doom_atan_cross_dot
distributions.description: atan2(cross/dot) over [-2, 2] × [0.5, 10] on non-uniform grid — mirrors the `atan_front_*` callsite at `torchwright/doom/stages/sorted.py:549` (rank-3 SVD).

ops.name: max
ops.notes: Exact via `(a + b + |a - b|) / 2`.

distributions.name: minmax_uniform_pm50
distributions.description: Two independent uniform streams on [-50, 50]². `min`/`max` are exact via the abs-trick; residual is float32 round-off only.

ops.name: min
ops.notes: Exact via `(a + b - |a - b|) / 2`.

distributions.name: minmax_uniform_pm50
distributions.description: Two independent uniform streams on [-50, 50]². `min`/`max` are exact via the abs-trick; residual is float32 round-off only.

ops.name: mod_const
ops.notes: Identity `x % d = x - d · thermometer_floor_div(x, d)`. Exact on integer inputs (the input class this op is declared for).

distributions.name: mod_integers_0_100_by7
distributions.description: Integer inputs on [0, 100] with divisor=7. `mod_const` is x − divisor × `thermometer_floor_div(x)`, exact on integers.

ops.name: multiply_2d
ops.notes: Single-sublayer product via 2D piecewise-linear lookup. Analytical worst-cell absolute bound is `step1*step2/4 = 0.25` for this configuration.

distributions.name: multiply_uniform_pm10
distributions.description: a·b over [-10, 10]² with step=1 — generic uniform product grid; analytical bound `step1*step2/4 = 0.25`.

ops.name: multiply_integers
ops.notes: Polarization + `square` on non-negative integer inputs. Exact on integer inputs by construction (the polarization identity is exact; `square` is exact at integer grid points).

distributions.name: multiply_integers_0_10
distributions.description: Integer pairs in [0, 10] — `multiply_integers` is exact on integer inputs by construction; serves as a precision-parity check.

ops.name: piecewise_linear
ops.notes: Foundation 1D piecewise-linear primitive; measured on the canonical x² test function with 11 integer breakpoints. Error is zero at breakpoints and bounded by the interpolation error of the reference function over each segment.

distributions.name: parabola_0_10_step1
distributions.description: f(x)=x² on [0, 10] with integer breakpoints — probes generic `piecewise_linear` precision between grid points.

ops.name: piecewise_linear_2d
ops.notes: Triangulated-grid lookup with non-uniform breakpoints. On non-uniform grids `piecewise_linear_2d` uses a constrained least-squares fit that can oscillate in cell interiors — `low_rank_2d` is preferred there.

distributions.name: doom_diff_trig
distributions.description: wall-geometry product over DIFF_BP × TRIG_BP — mirrors the `sort_ey_cos`/`sort_ex_sin` family of calls at `torchwright/doom/stages/wall.py:263` and neighbours.

distributions.name: doom_diff_vel
distributions.description: collision product over DIFF_BP × VEL_BP — mirrors the `p_dx_ey`/`p_dy_ex` family at `torchwright/doom/stages/wall.py:205`.

ops.name: reciprocal
ops.notes: Geometric breakpoint spacing keeps relative interpolation error roughly constant across the range. Measured against a reciprocal graph whose `(min_value, max_value, step)` matches its production callsite: `(0.3, 200.0, 1.0)` for `doom_reciprocal_wall` (`torchwright/doom/stages/wall.py:431`).

distributions.name: doom_reciprocal_wall
distributions.description: 1/x over [0.3, 200.0], step=1.0 — mirrors the `inv_abs_num_t` callsite at `torchwright/doom/stages/wall.py:339`.

ops.name: signed_multiply
ops.notes: Polarization identity `a·b = (|a+b|² - |a-b|²)/4`. Absolute error scales with `step × (max_abs1 + max_abs2)`, not with `|a·b|`; pathological near-zero × large-magnitude inputs exhibit high relative error.

distributions.name: signed_multiply_pm10
distributions.description: a·b over [-10, 10]² via polarization identity, step=1.0 — analytical bound `step × (max_abs1 + max_abs2) / 4 = 5.0` absolute, much smaller relative.

ops.name: square
ops.notes: Always an underestimate of x² between grid points. Max segment error is `(step/2)²` at half-integers.

distributions.name: square_unsigned_0_10
distributions.description: x² over [0, 10] with step=1.0 — exact at integers, worst error at half-integers.

ops.name: square_signed
ops.notes: Same interpolation profile as `square` but spans [-max_abs, max_abs]; saves one MLP sublayer vs. abs+square in `multiply_integers` / `signed_multiply` deep strategies.

distributions.name: square_signed_pm10
distributions.description: x² over [-10, 10] with step=1.0 — covers the negative half the unsigned variant cannot reach.

ops.name: thermometer_floor_div
ops.notes: Integer-input-only staircase. Ramps sit at `k*divisor - 0.5`, giving clean separation for integer inputs. Feeding continuous floats produces ramp-zone interpolation artefacts — those belong to `floor_int`, not this op.

distributions.name: thermometer_integers_0_100_by10
distributions.description: Integer inputs on [0, 100] with divisor=10 — `thermometer_floor_div` is defined only for integer inputs.
